revisedPrompt:
Use case: game asset sprite sheet
Asset type: SNES-style 16-bit pixel-art field prop / inventory icon
Output: exactly one PNG image. Exact pixel canvas: 32x32px. Grid: 1 row x 1 column, one 32x32px frame only.

Create a crisp low-resolution pixel-art inventory icon of the ritual short sword "harai-gatana" for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG. The icon is a short sword in a clean WHITE scabbard, wrapped with a thin golden sacred rope and two tiny white paper streamers. Add a vermilion cord on the hilt. Draw the sword diagonally across the single frame, large and readable, filling most of the 32x32 canvas while leaving a small clear margin on every edge.

Style/medium: authentic 16-bit pixel art, crisp square pixels, no antialiasing blur, consistent cheerful palette, one-pixel dark outline where useful, top-left light source, fine readable pixel clusters.
Composition/framing: one centered diagonal prop only, clear readable silhouette, clear collision footprint, no detached parts outside the main object.
Background: every empty pixel must be solid uniform #FF00FF magenta chroma key, no gradients, no transparency, no shadows, no texture.

Hard constraints: keep all artwork inside the single 32x32 cell; no cell separator lines, no grid lines, no frame border, no frame numbers, no labels, no text, no watermark. No isolated pixel islands, seam slivers, dust specks, or leftover effect residue. Leave clear solid #FF00FF margin between the sword and every canvas edge.
Avoid: no recognizable existing video-game franchise characters, logos, or likenesses; no samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, gun, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, bird, companion creature, letter emblems, modern UI, or watermark.
